title: The Vancouver Sun - August 14, 1981 (Page 122)
author: Unknown
card_summary: A newspaper clipping from The Vancouver Sun (Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada), dated August 14, 1981, page 122. This clipping dates to the final year of Philip K. Dick's life and may relate to Canadian reception of his work or coverage of SF community events.
page_summary:
A scanned newspaper page from The Vancouver Sun (Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada), dated Friday, August 14, 1981, page 122. The clipping was sourced from Newspapers.com and downloaded on April 15, 2025. The PDF text layer contains only source metadata with no recoverable article text.

Vancouver holds special significance in Dick's biography: he delivered his landmark speech "The Android and the Human" at the Vancouver Science Fiction Convention in 1972 and briefly considered relocating to Canada. This 1981 clipping dates from the final year of Dick's life (he died in March 1982) and may contain coverage related to Dick's Canadian connections or the broader science fiction community in Vancouver. The high page number suggests placement in a weekend supplement or special section.
